steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The underlined sentence says the predictive model “is sure to be invaluable in determining the optimal mix of a city’s amenities.” The question asks what function this sentence serves in the text as a whole.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘It summarizes trends in recent urban development in Boston.’: Boston appears only as an example of a neighborhood. The sentence says nothing about urban development trends. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘It emphasizes the potential utility of the team's model.’: “Is sure to be invaluable” directly asserts the model’s future usefulness. That is exactly what this choice describes. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘It praises an algorithm's accuracy in identifying neighborhood boundaries.’: The sentence is about the predictive model, not the clustering algorithm, and says nothing about accuracy in boundary identification. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘It suggests a difficulty associated with analyzing neighborhoods that the research team attempted to overcome.’: The sentence is confident and forward-looking, not describing a problem. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
